why_we_would: P3 is the one player directly accusing P0, so eliminating them neutralizes the counter-narrative against us before it can snowball, and in 6% of worlds it ends the game immediately with a perfect score. Crucially, the path is resilient in the dominant world: if P2 is the wolf (P=0.87), the night freeze removes P1 and the renormalized belief still lands on P2 on Day 2, so roughly 93% of the probability mass ends with the wolf caught rather than a wolf win. It trades a fast win for self-preservation plus a delayed but likely conviction.
risks: With 94% probability we knowingly lynch an innocent on Day 1 against our own dossier and hand the wolf a free night kill; in the P1 and P4 worlds (7% combined) that mistake is unrecoverable and the wolf wins outright. Even the most likely outcome is only a late catch (0.6), leaving the branch's ceiling low relative to its expected value of 0.582.
